title: cornus alba var. kesselringii
intro: Cornus alba var. kesselringii, also known as Tatarian dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Russia and Eastern Europe, characterized by its bright red winter stems and white flowers in spring. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to be used as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, with compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids contributing to its therapeutic effects. The health benefits of Cornus alba var. kesselringii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Cornus alba var. kesselringii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other topical applications.
